Off Calendar Oct-31-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
D. David Steele, Esq., of YARON & ASSOCIATES (415-658-2929), appearing for defendant / moving party Todd Shipyards Corporation
James P. Nevin, Esq., and Jason Rose, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Jack Olson

RULING:
DEFENDANT TODD SHIPYARDS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Todd Shipyards Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Olson was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant under their premises owner/contractor liability cause of action. Defendant's failure to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses to written contention discovery precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia O. Rodriguez, CSR # 12436 = (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-31-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lisa L. Oberg, Esq. of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
James P. Nevin, Esq., and Jason Rose, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Jack Olson

RULING:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 2 and 4 (strict liability) and denied as to issue 6 (punitive damages), which were the only issues defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. As to summary judgment, assuming without deciding that California Worker's Compensation law applies, there is a triable issue of material fact whether a special employment relationship existed between Mr. Olson and defendant and thus whether plaintiff's claims against defendant are barred by the Worker's Compensation Act. As to strict liability, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Olson was exposed to asbestos-containing products manufactured, distributed, sold, supplied or otherwise placed into the stream of commerce by defendant. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Diana L. Gonzalez, CSR # 7935 = (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-27-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jasun C. Molinelli, Esq., of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party Albay Construction Company
Christopher Hersom, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff
/ opposing party Jack Olson

RULING:
DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Albay Construction Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and punitive damages and off calendar as to strict liability. As to negligence, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Olson was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. Mr. Olson's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff's interrogatory responses included cites to and summarizes of various documents and evidence they alleged supported their contentions against defendant, and defendant's separate statement failed to discuss those items or demonstrate that they do not support plaintiffs' contentions. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Defendant's moving papers failed to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery on plaintiffs seeking all facts, witnesses and documents supporting plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages, or that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses thereto. Therefore defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar as to punitive damages. Plaintiffs have dismissed their strict liability cause of action. (Prevailing party is to prepare an order.) Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)
